classification_rationale: Validates ETL-layer enforcement ensuring downstream data pipelines do not propagate an invalid cancelled-to-shipped status change.
preconditions: ETL pipeline order_status_sync is deployed and scheduled in UAT environment.
Source staging table stg_order_status_updates contains a record: order_id = 'ORD-50321', current_status = 'CANCELLED', new_status = 'SHIPPED', event_timestamp = '2024-12-10T14:30:00Z'.
Target table dim_order_status is accessible for verification.
steps: Trigger execution of the order_status_sync ETL pipeline.
Wait for the pipeline run to complete.
Query the dim_order_status table for order_id = 'ORD-50321'.
Query the etl_rejection_log table for order_id = 'ORD-50321' and pipeline_run_id of the current execution.
expected_result: The dim_order_status table shows order ORD-50321 status remains CANCELLED. The etl_rejection_log contains a record with order_id ORD-50321, rejection_reason 'INVALID_STATE_TRANSITION: CANCELLED to SHIPPED not permitted', and the current pipeline_run_id.

ac_text: Cancelled orders cannot move to shipped.
title: Rapid consecutive API requests to transition cancelled order ORD-50321 to Shipped are all rejected consistently
classification_rationale: Validates that the business rule holds under concurrent request conditions, ensuring no race condition allows the invalid transition.
preconditions: API endpoint PATCH /api/v1/orders/{orderId}/status is available.
Order ORD-50321 exists with status Cancelled.
Bearer token for user ops_user_01 (role: Operations_Manager) is valid.
steps: Prepare 5 identical PATCH requests to /api/v1/orders/ORD-50321/status with body {"status": "Shipped"}.
Send all 5 requests simultaneously using a parallel HTTP client.
Collect all 5 responses.
Query the orders table for order ORD-50321 status.
expected_result: All 5 responses return HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity with error code INVALID_STATE_TRANSITION. The orders table confirms ORD-50321 status remains CANCELLED. No partial or inconsistent state is observed.

defect_detection.reason: Designed to catch failures or misbehavior

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: Order status change from Processing to Shipped is visible in Order Status Report and triggers email notification to customer
classification_rationale: Validates the core business rule that a status update propagates to both reporting and notifications within the Order Management module.
preconditions: Order ORD-20250101 exists with current status 'Processing' for customer CUST-10045 (Jane Doe, email: jane.doe@example.com)
User account ops_user_01 exists with role Operations_Manager and has update permission on Order Status module
Notification service is configured to send email notifications on status transitions
Order Status Report is accessible from the Reports module at /reports/order-status
steps: Navigate to Order Detail page for order ORD-20250101
Select 'Shipped' from the Status dropdown
Enter Carrier as 'FedEx' and Tracking Number as 'FDX-998877665'
Click the 'Update Status' button
Navigate to Reports > Order Status Report
Filter the report by Order ID ORD-20250101
Open the Notification Log for order ORD-20250101
expected_result: Order ORD-20250101 status displays as 'Shipped' on the Order Detail page. The Order Status Report shows a row for ORD-20250101 with status 'Shipped', timestamp matching the update time, and updated_by 'ops_user_01'. An email notification record exists for jane.doe@example.com with subject containing 'ORD-20250101' and body containing status 'Shipped' and tracking number 'FDX-998877665'.

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.

classification_rationale: Security-focused validation ensuring unauthorized users cannot trigger status updates that would appear in reporting or notifications.

Reporting and notification services are online

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: Rapid consecutive status updates within 2 seconds both appear in reporting and both trigger separate notifications
classification_rationale: Tests a boundary condition where rapid sequential updates could cause race conditions in reporting or notification deduplication.
preconditions: Order ORD-20250707 exists with current status 'Pending' for customer CUST-10088 (Sarah White, email: sarah.white@example.com)
API authentication token is valid for service account svc_operations with scope order:update
Notification and reporting services are online
steps: Send PUT request to /api/v1/orders/ORD-20250707/status with the first update body
Immediately send PUT request to /api/v1/orders/ORD-20250707/status with the second update body within 2 seconds of the first
Send GET request to /api/v1/reports/order-status?order_id=ORD-20250707
Send GET request to /api/v1/notifications?order_id=ORD-20250707&type=status_change
expected_result: Both PUT requests return HTTP 200. Reporting endpoint returns two distinct records for ORD-20250707: one with status 'Processing' at '2025-07-15T12:00:00Z' and one with status 'Shipped' at '2025-07-15T12:00:01Z'. Notifications endpoint returns two separate notification entries for sarah.white@example.com, one for each status transition. Final order status is 'Shipped'.
